user_query:
I'm prepping for a short teaching-team sync after our "Searing & Maillard" lab. Please create two deliverables from the local materials:

1) A meeting notes document at output/meeting_notes.md
2) An action items CSV at output/action_items.csv

Use and cross-reference these inputs:
- input/lesson_outline.md (course content)
- input/lab_observations.txt (last lab observations)
- config/lab_settings.yaml (lab metadata and equipment settings)
- scripts/logger.py (our simple temp logger script)

Requirements:
A) Read and extract relevant content
- From input/lesson_outline.md: For each top-level section under the lesson outline (e.g., Maillard Reaction, Caramelization, Protein Denaturation and Coagulation), capture the heading and the first explanatory sentence immediately following it.
- From input/lab_observations.txt: Capture all lines beginning with "ISSUE:" as discussion points.

B) Inspect code and configuration files to surface issues
- Compare numeric parameters between scripts/logger.py and config/lab_settings.yaml for:
  • Sous-vide target temperature
  • Calibration offset (Celsius)
  • Oil smoke point (Celsius)
  Create an item for every mismatch discovered (only mismatches; skip if values match). Include both values and their sources.
- Extract every TODO and FIXME comment from both scripts/logger.py and config/lab_settings.yaml.

C) Prepare meeting notes and action items
- output/meeting_notes.md should include:
  • Title line with meeting_title and meeting_date from config/lab_settings.yaml (lab.meeting_title and lab.meeting_date)
  • A "Teaching Highlights" section listing each lesson topic with its one-sentence summary from the outline
  • A "Lab Issues from Config/Code" section summarizing:
    - each parameter mismatch with file names and values (code vs config)
    - each TODO/FIXME note found, with file references
  • A "Discussion Points from Observations" section listing all captured ISSUE lines from input/lab_observations.txt
  • An "Action Items" section that mirrors the rows written to the CSV (IDs and brief descriptions are sufficient)

- output/action_items.csv should have columns:
  id,description,source_file,category,due_date,priority
  Where:
  • id: A1, A2, ... in sequence
  • description: concise statement of the mismatch or TODO/FIXME or observation issue
  • source_file: one of scripts/logger.py, config/lab_settings.yaml, or input/lab_observations.txt
  • category: one of mismatch, todo, fixme, observation_issue
  • due_date: lab.meeting_date + 3 days, in YYYY-MM-DD (ISO) format
  • priority: High for mismatch, fixme, and observation_issue; Medium for todo

Additional notes:
- Treat lab.meeting_date in config/lab_settings.yaml as the canonical meeting date for the document header and due date calculation.
- Keep the meeting notes concise but include all identified mismatches and TODO/FIXME items, and all observation issues.
- Do not modify any input files; create only the two specified outputs under the output/ directory. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
